questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You have taken up a project to create night shelters for homeless people during the winter season. Within a week of establishing the shelters, you have received complaints from the residents of the area about the increase in theft cases with a demand to remove the shelters. You would

choices.a: ask them to lodge a written complaint in the police station
choices.b: assure residents of an enquiry into the matter
choices.c: ask residents to consider the humanitarian effort made
choices.d: continue with the project and ignore their complaint

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You have a pharmaceutical company. You have received information that someone who is not an employee has tampered with a certain type of tablets in a specific area, which has caused some deaths in that area. In this type of crisis, what will you do?

choices.a: This incident can have a negative effect on your company's reputation and earnings and can lead to loss. In order to avoid this you don't launch a campaign to alert the people
choices.b: Launch a campaign to alert the public and recall tablets from the specific area
choices.c: Recall tablets from only the specific area and not the whole country
choices.d: Recall those tablets from the entire country despite the fact that the tampering of tablets occurred in a certain area

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: While sitting in a park, you observe that a smart young man comes to the place on a scooter, leaves it there and goes away with someone else on a motorbike. You would

choices.d: inform the police at the nearby booth

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: A person lives in a far off village which is almost two hours by bus. The villager's neighbour is a very powerful landlord who is trying to occupy the poor villager's land by force. You are the District Magistrate and have been called by a postmaster. The villager has come all the way, by bus and on foot, to see you and give an application seeking protection from the powerful landlord. The villager keeps on waiting outside the meeting hall for an hour. You come out of the meeting and are rushing to another meeting. The villager follows you to submit his application. What would you do?

choices.a: Tell him to wait for another two hours till you come back from your next meeting
choices.b: Tell him that the matter is actually to be dealt by a junior officer and that he should give the application to him
choices.c: Call one of your senior subordinate officers and ask him to solve the villager's problem
choices.d: Quickly take the application from him, ask him a few relevant questions regarding his problem and then proceed to the meeting

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are moving across the road on a scooter when you observe that two boys on a bike snatch a lady's gold chain and side away. You would

choices.b: chase the boys to catch hold of them
choices.c: inform the police about the matter

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: On reaching the railway station, you find that the train you wanted to catch is just to start and there is hardly any time for purchasing the ticket. The best thing for you is to

choices.a: rush to the train rather than miss it and inform the T.T.I. at the next stoppage about your inability to purchase the ticket
choices.b: rush to the train and perform your journey quietly
choices.c: first purchase the ticket and then catch the train, if it is there
choices.d: miss the train than take the risk of boarding the moving train

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: A train is coming and you are standing at the station. Suddenly you notice that the railway track is broken. You will

choices.a: inform the authority to take necessary action
choices.c: use some means to stop train immediately

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: You are drinking your car on the road when you hit against a fruit vendor's cart. You would

choices.a: escape from the site by driving away
choices.b: abuse the fruit vendor for putting his cart on the way
choices.c: pay the fruit vendor for the damage done to him
choices.d: insist that it was not your fault
